strategies.name: ICT Risk Management
strategies.description: ICT risk management framework encompassing governance arrangements, identification of ICT-supported business functions, protection and prevention measures, detection of anomalous activities, and response and recovery procedures.

ML1.summary: Establish foundational ICT risk governance, restrict third-party app consent, enable audit logging, and enforce baseline identity protection controls.

ML2.summary: Deploy comprehensive conditional access policies, advanced identity protection, network segmentation, endpoint compliance, and data classification controls aligned to ICT risk assessments.

ML3.summary: Enable advanced ICT risk controls including device compliance enforcement, real-time risk-based sign-in policies, DLP protections, and continuous monitoring for anomalous activities.

strategies.name: ICT-Related Incident Management
strategies.description: Classification and reporting of ICT-related incidents, including detection capabilities, incident response procedures, notification to competent authorities, and harmonised reporting templates.

ML1.summary: Onboard devices to Defender for Endpoint, enable core detection capabilities, configure tamper protection, and establish baseline alert notification workflows.

ML2.summary: Enable EDR in block mode, automated investigation and remediation, network protection, web content filtering, attack surface reduction rules, and advanced threat detection capabilities.

ML3.summary: Deploy deception technology, threat intelligence indicators, insider risk detection, and advanced incident classification aligned to DORA reporting thresholds.

strategies.name: Digital Operational Resilience Testing
strategies.description: Testing of ICT tools, systems and processes through vulnerability assessments, network security assessments, scenario-based testing, and threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) for critical financial entities.

ML1.summary: Establish baseline audit logging, vulnerability scanning, and retention policies to support resilience testing evidence collection and gap analysis.

ML2.summary: Configure advanced testing controls including attack surface reduction, automated remediation, and scenario-based policy validation for operational resilience.

ML3.summary: Enable eDiscovery, records management, and regulatory hold capabilities to support threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) documentation and evidence preservation.

strategies.name: ICT Third-Party Risk Management
strategies.description: Managing risks arising from ICT third-party service providers, including key contractual provisions, oversight of critical third-party providers, and the Union Oversight Framework.

ML1.summary: Restrict third-party application consent, enforce admin consent workflows, and establish foundational external collaboration controls.

ML2.summary: Configure SharePoint and Teams external sharing policies, guest access restrictions, and cross-tenant access controls for third-party risk oversight.

ML3.summary: Deploy advanced third-party governance including sensitivity-labelled sites, customer lockbox, information barriers, and comprehensive external sharing auditing.

strategies.name: Information Sharing Arrangements
strategies.description: Voluntary sharing of cyber threat intelligence and information amongst financial entities, including arrangements for exchanging indicators of compromise, tactics, techniques, and procedures.

ML1.summary: Deploy sensitivity labels and baseline data protection policies to enable secure classification and sharing of threat intelligence information.

ML2.summary: Configure auto-labeling, DLP policies, secure mail transport rules, and Exchange Online protection controls for regulated information exchange.

ML3.summary: Enable insider risk management, endpoint DLP, information barriers, communication compliance, and advanced encryption for comprehensive information sharing governance.
